title: A Brave Orangutan Crosses a New Bridge
summary: In Indonesia, a road was built through a forest where orangutans live. This split the orangutan community into two groups. Scientists worried that if the groups could not meet, they might have health problems from inbreeding and could even disappear forever. To help, conservation groups and the government built a special bridge high up in the trees, called a canopy bridge. They hoped the orangutans would use it to cross the road safely. For two years, no orangutan used the bridge. But then, one brave orangutan finally crossed it! This was very exciting for the people who built the bridge. Now, the orangutans can visit each other again, which will help keep the community healthy.

keywords.explanation: a large ape with long arms that lives in trees
a group of animals or people living together
when closely related animals have babies, which can cause health problems
when all animals of a kind die out
a bridge built high in the trees for animals to cross safely

background_read: Orangutans are great apes that live in the rainforests of Indonesia and Malaysia. They spend most of their time in trees and are known for their long arms and reddish-brown hair. Roads built through forests can make it hard for animals to move around safely. Canopy bridges are one way to help animals cross roads without getting hurt.
Article_Structure: WHAT: A brave orangutan crossed a new canopy bridge
WHERE: In a forest in Indonesia's Sumatra
WHY: To reach the other side of the community split by a road

perspectives.description: They built the bridge to help orangutans stay healthy.
They can now safely cross the road to meet other orangutans.
